que: Which of the following is an example of an Android configuration change?
What is the default Android behavior for most configuration changes?
options: The activity is destroyed and recreated

que: Why does Android usually recreate activities on configuration changes?
options: To reload resources appropriate for the new configuration

que: What is a major risk if your app does not handle recreation correctly?
options: Loss of UI state like text input or scroll position

que: What does Android recommend about `android:configChanges` for most apps?
options: Avoid using it unless you truly need to handle changes manually
Use it on every activity by default

que: Which of the following is a good practice for configuration changes?
options: Store longer-lived screen state in a ViewModel
Store all important state only in activity fields

que: Which user action can trigger a configuration change?
What is a common mistake with configuration changes?
options: Relying on activity fields as the only source of important screen state
Letting the system recreate the activity

que: Why are configuration changes especially important on large-screen devices?
options: Because resizing and multi-window changes can happen more often
Because they do not support activities
Because they use a different Kotlin language

que: Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Handle configuration changes and configuration continuity docs
